request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the CLARITY Act be signed into law in 2026?
Kind: active
Timeline (6 events):
- 2026-05-19 [A] CFTC Sues Minnesota to Block State Law — CFTC suing Minnesota to block state crypto law suggests federal enforcement can resolve jurisdictional disputes, reducing pressure to enact CLARITY Act.
- 2026-05-06 [background] CDNA - Amendments to rules on Insider definition, jurisdiction… - 05/06/2026 — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-05-01 [background] 05.01.2026 - Restructure the Disciplinary Panel and Appeals Committee from standing committees to ad hoc committees convened on an as-needed basis. - GMNI — Internal CFTC committee restructuring is administrative and unlikely to affect congressional passage or presidential signing of H.R.3633.
- 2026-05-01 [background] 05.01.2026 - Block Trade Rebate Program - KEX — CFTC's block-trade rebate action for Kalshi is an administrative market move unrelated to passage of H.R.3633, so it gives no clear signal.
- 2026-04-16 [background] COIN | Non-Crypto Market Maker Program - 04/16/2026 — CFTC's Coinbase market-maker program shows regulator activity on crypto markets but doesn't materially change likelihood of H.R.3633 passage.
- 2026-04-16 [background] COIN | Modifications to trading hours… - 04/16/2026 — Routine CFTC action on Coinbase trading hours is background regulatory activity and does not materially change odds that H.R.3633 will be enacted.

response.text: The CFTC’s May suit against Minnesota to block a state crypto law signals regulators can handle jurisdictional clashes, easing near-term pressure to advance the CLARITY Act. Other CFTC moves (committee changes, rebates, market-maker programs, trading-hour tweaks) are routine and largely non-directional. Passage by Congress and a 2026 presidential signature remain pending.
